title: project manager path placement
description: A short quiz on the core ideas of the Project Manager path. Answer what you know. Each chapter you place out of is one you can skip ahead on, and you can revisit any chapter any time.

scoring.chapterSkipRule: Each chapter is tested by 2 questions worth 2 points each. A learner places out of a chapter (skips its Version A) by answering both of its questions correctly. Overall, 50 percent or more of maxScore recommends Version B as the default for chapters not individually skipped.

questions.prompt: Your team is scoping a feature that calls an AI model. What is an API call, in plain terms?

options.label: One request from your code to the model, which has a cost and a response time
A meeting with the AI vendor
A one-time setup that then runs free forever
A type of bug report

questions.prompt: A feature you are scoping will call an AI model once per user action, for thousands of users a day. What belongs in the plan?

options.label: Cost and response time per call, since both add up at that volume
Nothing, model calls are free and instant at any scale
Only the visual design
A plan to call the model as few times as one per month

questions.prompt: What does writing a good prompt for an AI model most resemble?

options.label: Writing a clear brief: context, the goal, constraints, and what success looks like
Writing code in a secret language
Guessing magic words
Nothing, prompts cannot be planned

questions.prompt: An AI tool gave a vague, off-target result from the instruction "make this better." What is the fix?

options.label: Rewrite the instruction to say what 'better' means here: the goal, the constraints, an example
Send "make this better" again, louder
Accept the vague result
Conclude the model cannot help

questions.prompt: An AI agent finished a task. It left behind a trace. What is a trace?

options.label: A step-by-step record of what the agent did: what it read, decided, and acted on
A leftover error that has to be cleaned up
The agent's final answer, with nothing else
A type of chart

questions.prompt: An AI feature produced a wrong result and you need to know why. How does the trace help?

options.label: You read the trace to see which step went wrong, instead of guessing
It does not help, traces are random
It tells you the result is fine after all
Traces only exist for successful runs

questions.prompt: Your team wants to know whether an AI feature is good enough to ship. What actually answers that?

options.label: Evals: running the feature against many real example cases and scoring the results
One demo that happened to go well
Asking the model if it thinks it is good
The size of the model

questions.prompt: "It worked when I tried it once." Why is that not enough to trust an AI feature?

options.label: AI output varies, so one good result does not show it works across the range of real cases
It is enough, one success proves it always works
Trying it once is actually too many tests
AI features never need testing
